texto: I'm really passionate about pantomime because it is often the first introduction for a child to theatre, and if that child has a great experience at a pantomime they will continue to come year after year.

texto: I am who I am firstly because of genetics, and, running a very close second, because of choices: ones my parents made, such as choosing to emigrate to America; ones their parents made, like my Papa Butler opting to ignore medical advice and instead warming my mum in the oven to keep her alive; and very conscious ones that I've made for myself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Barrowman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I am who I am firstly because of genetics, and, running a very close second, because of choices: …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, personal-growth, because pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: Yet if I was asked to do this again - in fact, if I was ever asked to repeat any of my experiences - I'd have to say, fuck it, bring them on. I've no regrets.This is what it means to be alive.

texto: Many things shaped my identity as a young boy: a strong selfworth (something that was instilled in all three Barrowman siblings by our parents), my immersion in theatre and music, and my DNA. I was born gay. It's not a choice I – or anyone else who is gay – made. If it were, why on earth would anyone choose to be part of a minority, part of a group that in so many cultures and countries, even in the twenty-first century, is regularly blasphemed, hounded and worse?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Barrowman que provoca temas ligados a autoestima. Ao questionar “Many things shaped my identity as a young boy: a strong selfworth (something that was instilled i…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre autoestima, john-barrowman, sexuality, com foco em sexuality.
